title: Conflict and Humanitarian Crises: Research & Analysis | CSIS
text:
Conflict and Humanitarian Crises

CSIS analyzes the successes and challenges of humanitarian crisis response in regions facing violence, climate disaster, or other human security issues around the world.

Subsidies, Stockpiles, and Shortages

This installment of Charting the Middle East spotlights regional government responses to the Iran war’s widening impacts.

Newsletter
by
Josiane Atallah

— June 18, 2026

State of Play

Trump's Iran Deal: What Comes Next?

U.S. and Iran announce a framework deal with a 60‑day ceasefire and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. CSIS experts analyze its winners, challenges ahead, and its impact on the Middle East.

Podcast Episode
by
CSIS Scholars

— June 15, 2026

Image

Iran's Food Crisis

This installment of Charting the Middle East explores how the war has exacerbated Iran's economic collapse and its cost-of-living crisis.

Newsletter
by
Lilya Yatim

— June 12, 2026

How Should the United States Counter the CRINK Axis?

Please join a special live episode of State of Play and The Impossible State to explore strategies to counter the CRINK axis.

Event

— June 11, 2026

Fragility, Conflict, and the Future of Aid in Turbulent Times

CSIS will examine the future of aid amid rising conflict and global disorder. Experts will discuss how displacement, changing aid dynamics, and political instability impact fragile states, exploring how organizations must adapt to growing demand and constrained resources.

Event

— June 8, 2026

Navigating Lebanon's Troubled Economic Recovery: A Conversation with Lebanon's Minister of Economy and Trade, H.E. Amer Bisat

Lebanon's Minister of Economy and Trade H.E. Amer Bisat joins Paul Salem and Mona Yacoubian to discuss Lebanon's economic recovery.

Event

— June 8, 2026

Fragility, Conflict, and the Future of Aid in Turbulent Times

The Global Development Department hosted a conversation on fragility, conflict, and the future of aid in turbulent times.

Transcript

— June 8, 2026

Nicaragua Under Repression: Human Rights, Accountability, and Exile

Join the Americas Program for a special hybrid event, featuring Jan Michael Simon, Chair of the U.N. Group of Human Rights Experts on Nicaragua (GHREN), to unpack key findings from GHREN’s March 2026 report and examine accountability, international response, and the future of human rights in Nicaragua.

Event

— May 29, 2026

The Vatican Just Outmaneuvered Washington on AI

The Vatican’s AI intervention exposes Washington’s blind spot: Policymakers are debating hypothetical doomsday scenarios while missing the real crises already reshaping human dignity, work, truth, and power. Washington must move beyond abstract AGI fears and build a civil society dialogue on human dignity, supply chains, truth, and statecraft.

Commentary
by
Benjamin Jensen

— May 26, 2026

Into Africa

Beyond Aid: Redefining Africa’s Development

Tjada D'Oyen McKenna, CEO of Mercy Corps, and Noam Unger, Vice-President of the Global Development department at CSIS, join Oge to assess the current state of the international development and humanitarian assistance landscape and explore what challenges and opportunities this moment presents for Africa’s future. 

Podcast Episode
by

Oge Onubogu
and
Noam Unger

— April 23, 2026
